summary: Tang dynasty Chinese poet, one of the most celebrated poets in Chinese literature. Known for 'Drinking Alone by Moonlight,' 'Quiet Night Thought,' and numerous poems about wine, the moon, and nature. Also known as Li Po. Associated with Du Fu.

works.name: Drinking Alone by Moonlight
works.description: Famous poem about drinking wine with the moon and shadow as companions.

clues.clue: Speaker drinks alone, invites the moon and his shadow to join — making a party of three
examples: a poem about drinking alone with the moon

clues.clue: Moon and shadow as drinking companions
examples: the moon and shadow serve as companions

works.name: Quiet Night Thought
works.description: Short poem about moonlight and homesickness.

clues.clue: 'Bright moonlight before my bed / I suspect it is frost on the ground'; looking up at moon, bowing head thinking of home
examples: looking at the moon and thinking of home

works.name: The River-Merchant's Wife: A Letter
works.description: Poem translated by Ezra Pound, about a young wife waiting for her husband.

clues.clue: Translated by Ezra Pound from Chinese; young wife describes growing up with merchant husband; waits for him to return

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Tang dynasty poet, associated with Du Fu, wine, and moonlight.

clues.clue: Tang dynasty poet; counterpart/friend of Du Fu; associated with wine and moon
Legend says he drowned trying to embrace the moon's reflection in water
examples: drowned trying to embrace the moon's reflection

clues.clue: Known as the 'Immortal of Poetry' or 'Banished Immortal'
Served briefly at court of Emperor Xuanzong; later exiled

comprehensive_summary:
Li Bai (701-762, also known as Li Po) was a Tang dynasty Chinese poet, one of the greatest in Chinese literary history. He is often paired with his friend and counterpart Du Fu. Known as the 'Banished Immortal,' he was associated with wine, moonlight, and nature.

'Drinking Alone by Moonlight' features the speaker drinking alone, inviting the moon and his shadow as companions to make a party of three. 'Quiet Night Thought' begins 'Bright moonlight before my bed' and ends with the speaker bowing his head thinking of home. 'The River-Merchant's Wife: A Letter' was famously translated by Ezra Pound.

Legend holds that he drowned trying to embrace the moon's reflection in water. He served briefly at the court of Emperor Xuanzong before being exiled.
